figures.caption: Fig. 3. Confusion matrix for Utterance Type (UT) classification (RoBERTabase +Aug, 10-class). Rows are true labels; columns are predicted labels.
TABLE I UTTERANCE TYPE (UT) CODING SCHEME (10 CLASSES).
Fig. 4. Utterance Type (UT)×Reasoning Component (RC) Co-occurrence Heatmap. Left: raw counts; Right: P (RC|UT) (row-normalized).
Fig. 5. Aggregated CCI Time Series across Lesson Progression (binned into 10 equal segments). Shaded region indicates ±1 standard deviation.

Fig. 6. 3-Turn IRF Patterns Ranked by Mean Student CCI (min. n=5, N=26 patterns). Error bars are session-level bootstrap 95% CIs. Bar color encodes CCI magnitude (red→green scale). Top-ranked: Fq→Rs→Fq (n=36, mean CCI=2.06).
TABLE IV CLASS DISTRIBUTION BY SPLIT (SESSION-LEVEL 6/2/1 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH PARTITION; N = 1,782). SESSION-LEVEL ASSIGNMENT PREVENTS CONTEXT-WINDOW DATA LEAKAGE. SUBTOTAL % IS OVER THE FULL CORPUS.

Fig. 12. Teacher-framing heatmap on pseudo-labeled data. Left: mean student CCI per (initiation UT × feedback UT) pair. Right: observation counts. Compare with Fig. 7 (human-labeled). CCI values are uniformly lower and the high-CCI peak at Fq×Q visible in the human-labeled heatmap is absent, reflecting the model’s suppression of SR-I predictions.

Fig. 10. 3-turn Teacher–Student–Teacher IRF patterns ranked by mean student CCI on pseudo-labeled data (min. n=5). Compare with Fig. 6 (humanlabeled). Overall CCI values are lower and the gap between top- and bottomranked patterns is narrower, consistent with the model under-predicting higherorder RC classes.
Fig. 7. Teacher-framing heatmap. Left: mean student CCI per (initiation UT×feedback UT) pair (grey = fewer than 3 observations). Right: observation count per cell.

Fig. 9. Aggregated CCI over lesson progression computed on pseudo-labeled data (model predictions). Compare with Fig. 5 (human-labeled): the sharp terminal rebound visible in the human-labeled trajectory is absent here.

Fig. 2. Dual-Probe Head (DPH) architecture. Each of the 2k+1 context utterances is individually wrapped as ⟨s⟩di⟨/s⟩ before concatenation into the encoder input. For the target utterance dt (highlighted), the <s> BOS hidden state h (t) ⟨s⟩ serves as the UT probe and the </s> EOS hidden state h (t) ⟨/s⟩ serves as the RC probe. Dashed arrows denote cross-conditioning: the UT head receives [u; r] and the RC head receives [r; u].
